text:
Much like a beast that secretly spreads its nest underground,
Parallelmon expands their territory while hiding in pockets of
space-time.

One might say that this vast corridor is a literal pathway
through space-time itself. It seems to have grown twisted,
as if weaving through the very gaps in time and space.

Expanding indefinitely, connecting to endless dimensions...
Think of it like a terminal train station, with countless rail
lines all converging at a single point.

The station in Shinjuku often feels like a maze to those who
visit it for the first time, yes? It is much like that. I suppose
the difference is...

...if you were to ever get lost, the world would simply meet its
end...

The commonality between all of the Akashic debris we've seen
so far is that they all relate to the fathers of these two girls.

If similar memories continue to arise in the future, it may be
because there is something causing them to do so.

For example, the sentiments these girls have toward their
fathers—perhaps these memories are being drawn by these
feelings of "bond" and "love"?

I don't think that's the case with the girl known as Kyoko
Kuremi, though... In fact, I would say it's quite the opposite.

Kyoko Kuremi grew darker when she talked about her father.
The moment the bond between two people turns sour, it
becomes a connection that is difficult to break.

The ties that bind people are not always pleasant. When love
is no longer present, it gives way to the swirling vortex people
refer to as hatred.

Keep looking for Parallelmon. If things continue like this, we'll
be on a path to chaos.
